characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptational Nice Guy: Since this is a show meant for little kids, the Looneys are much sweeter and more innocent than their adult counterparts. This is especially true of Sylvester and Taz, who were often villains in the original shorts. The only exceptions are Lola (who has always been a nice character in all her incarnations) and Melissa, who gets a bit ofAdaptational Jerkassinstead. Annoying Laugh: One episode had them doing this at Sylvester's misfortunes. Ascended Extra: Petunia and especially Melissa were very minor characters in the prior Looney Tunes cartoons. Here, they are main characters. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: Lola Bunny, Petunia Pig and Melissa Duck in that order. Bows for Cuteness: Lola, Melissa, and Petunia all wear bows to represent their youth.

characters.name: Bugs Bunny
characters.description: The leader of the kids, and is implied to be the oldest. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptational Wimp: While he's probably one of the most resourceful babies, he's stillnothingcompared to adult Bugs. Notably he spends an entire episode worrying because Daffy was making fun of him and he needs Granny to comfort him at the end. Alliterative Name: His first and last name starts with B. Bunnies for Cuteness: Bugs Bunny's adult self was already pretty cute, but in this incarnation, it's taken even further! Expressive Ears: Bugs's rabbit ears go downwards when he's sad.

characters.name: Daffy Duck
characters.description: Second in the lead after Bugs. He's bratty but means well. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Alliterative Name: His surname and first name begin with D. Attention Whore: Much like almost all versions of the duck, Daffy loves to get noticed! Blatant Lies: After Melissa confesses her fibs about Taz, he tries to say that he knew they were lies the whole time. Of course, no one fell for that trick.Daffy:I knew those were fibs all along. I was just going along with it for the entertainment value.Everyone:(angrily) Huh-Huh!(Daffy turns to Tweety, who shakes his head, almost like saying "Nope, I'm not falling for that.") Bratty Half-Pint: You probably expected it from a younger Daffy Duck, didn't you?

characters.name: Tweety Bird
characters.description: The youngest of the children. See the original Mainline Tunehere. The Baby of the Bunch: He's the youngest of the babies. The Cutie: He's a cute birdie. Elmuh Fudd Syndwome: He talks like this. The actualTrope Namer, Elmer Fudd, even appears on this show. Feather Fingers: He has feathers that look like fingers.

characters.name: Lola Bunny
characters.description: Third in the lead after Daffy, a close friend of Bugs and the tomboy of the group. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adorably Precocious Child: She's shown to be a practical and responsible kid. Emphasized in the Italian dub where she's played byMonica Ward, the Italian voice ofBlossomandLisa Simpson. Badass Adorable: Downplayed, since this is a show for little kids, though she still is by far the most rough-and-tumble of the children. Bunnies for Cuteness: Just like Bugs. Distaff Counterpart: She's portrayed as a female Bugs.

characters.name: Sylvester Cat
characters.description: A shy and neurotic kitten who's the least similar from the adult version (although versions are a tad cowardly and both have speech impediments). See the original Mainline Tunehere. Attention Whore: He enjoys getting attention from Granny more than the others. "Sylvester The Pester" completely revolved around this. Butt-Monkey: Due to his lack of self-esteem. Cats Are Snarkers: Sylvester is prone to sarcastic quips. Cats Hate Water: It's a central plot point in "The Puddle Olympics".

characters.name: Taz
characters.description: Taz is a silly tasmanian devil. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Baths Are Fun: Learned this moral at the denouement of "All Washed Up". Big Eater: Just like his grown-up self. Big Fun: On his better days he's fun-loving, though he also cries easily. Bratty Half-Pint: In the early episodes, he had a tendency to throw a fit when things went wrong.

characters.name: Melissa Duck
characters.description: Introduced in the middle of the first season, she is a female Daffy Duck in some ways. Her best friend is Petunia Pig. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptational Jerkass: A little more brash in this cartoon than her adult counterpart. Justified, since her only appearance in the original shorts had her as nothing more than a damsel in distress. Adaptation Dye-Job: She has red hair rather than blonde like in the old shorts. Always Someone Better: As seen in one episode, she is better than Petunia at almost everything. Bratty Half-Pint: In herA Day in the Limelightepisodes, she's often shown to be a brat who is a bit annoying to the other kids, though she normally learns her lesson at the end.

characters.name: Petunia Pig
characters.description: Added to the show in the middle of season one. Her best friend is Melissa Duck. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptation Dye-Job: Petunia normally has black hair but here it is brown. Averted in Season 2 when it becomes black again. Alliterative Name: Both her first name and surname start with P. Curtains Match the Window: In Season 1, she had brown hair and brown eyes. Flat Character: Has the least characterization of the babies.

characters.name: Granny
characters.description: The caretaker of the children. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Cool Old Lady: A wise and motherly sort. Demoted to Extra: Appears less often in season 2. Friend to All Children: She deeply cares for the babies and is always sweet to all of them. Friend to All Living Things: She takes care of a group of animals. Downplayed since they are treated like normal children.

characters.name: Floyd Minton
characters.description: Granny's nephew, he looks after the kids when she's not around. Canon Foreigner: Didn't appear in the original shorts. Determinator: Determined not to let Granny down.

characters.name: Marvin the Martian
characters.description: A timid martian who only appears in "War Of The Weirds". See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptational Heroism: His portrayal in the shorts was sociopathic and evil, but this Marvin is a helpful and kind fella. Alien Among Us: His presence as an alien isn't brought up in the episode, just that he's "visiting for a while". However, the songs that he appears in explicitly bring up his Mars roots. All of the Other Reindeer: The rest of the kids except Taz didn't want to play with him because they thought he was weird. Break the Cutie: After being ditched by everyone else when they thought he was "weird".

characters.name: Pepe Le Pew
characters.description: Appears in both "New Cat in Town" and "Stop and Smell Up the Flowers". Tends to make odd smells. See the original Mainline Tunehere. The Baby of the Bunch: All the characters are babies, but in his first appearance, Pepe is a portrayed as a speechless infant who is younger than everyone else. Cute Mute: In his debut episode, he's too young to talk and gets all the attention for his cuteness. Funny Foreigner: He's French, much like his adult version. Ridiculously Cute Critter:Even by the standards of this show.

characters.name: Elmer Fudd
characters.description: Appears in "A Bully for Bugs" as a kid somewhat older than the babies, and as a baby in the songs. See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptational Danger Downgrade: Instead of being a hunter who wants to kill the main characters, he's instead a bully who robs them. Age Lift: Suddenly a kindergartener in the second and final season, much like the changes his older counterpart goes through ie Egghead. Berserk Button: You'd better pay attention to him when he speaks to you. Bratty Half-Pint: Makes Daffy look well-behaved.

characters.name: Foghorn Leghorn
characters.description: The central character of "Cock A Doodle Do It". See the original Mainline Tunehere. Adaptational Badass: He wins a challenge against the barnyard dog. Badass Adorable: A cute rooster who is good at swimming, plus is strong. Beware the Nice Ones: He tells off the dog for chasing the roosters around. Be Yourself: He learns this moral at the end of "Cock A Doodle Do It".
